dosing_bands.notes: 0.5 mL IM. TT or Td for wound prophylaxis. With TIG if tetanus-prone wound + incomplete immunization; Fixed volume per dose

contraindications: Severe allergic reaction to previous dose
History of Guillain-Barré syndrome within 6 weeks of prior tetanus vaccination

interactions.effect: May reduce immune response to vaccine; administer when possible before starting immunosuppression

pediatric_specific_warnings: Anterolateral thigh for infants; deltoid for older children
May cause local pain, swelling, fever for 1-2 days
Severe reactions (Arthus-type) if boosted too frequently

administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with other vaccines in same syringe unless specified
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C; do NOT freeze — freezing destroys vaccine potency

storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C (cold chain); do NOT freeze; protect from light; discard if frozen
lactation_note: Safe during lactation; recommended for maternal immunisation
reference_source: WHO Position Paper on Tetanus Vaccines

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=9 months (some brands >=12 months); IAP recommends routine vaccination at 15 months and 4-6 years

dosing_bands.duration_note: IAP: Dose 1 at 15 months, Dose 2 at 4-6 years. SC injection
dosing_bands.notes: 0.5 mL SC. Live vaccine. 2 doses. IAP schedule: 15m + 4-6y; Fixed volume per dose

contraindications: Immunodeficiency (primary or acquired — HIV with CD4 <15%)
Pregnancy (avoid pregnancy for 1 month after vaccination)
Anaphylaxis to previous dose or neomycin/gelatin
cross_reactions: Other live vaccines — space by 4 weeks or give simultaneously

interactions.effect: Avoid salicylates for 6 weeks post-vaccination — theoretical risk of Reye syndrome
Defer vaccination for 3-11 months after immunoglobulin (depending on product and dose)
May reduce vaccine efficacy — avoid antivirals for 14 days before and 21 days after vaccination

pediatric_specific_warnings: Live vaccine — do NOT give to immunocompromised children
Avoid salicylates for 6 weeks after vaccination
Vaccine-strain varicella may rarely be transmitted to susceptible contacts — counsel parents
Mild varicella-like rash in 3-5% is expected and self-limiting
Can be given simultaneously with MMR at different sites (or use MMRV)

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute lyophilised vaccine with provided diluent (0.5 mL SWFI); use within 30 min
administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with other vaccines in same syringe; can give simultaneously at different site
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C (or -20°C for long-term); protect from light; use within 30 min of reconstitution

storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C (cold chain) or -20°C for long-term; protect from light; reconstituted vaccine: use within 30 minutes
lactation_note: Vaccine-strain VZV not detected in breast milk in studies; safe during lactation
reference_source: WHO Position Paper on Varicella Vaccines

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages; used as diluent for reconstitution and dilution of parenteral medications

dosing_bands.notes: Sterile diluent only. NEVER infuse undiluted — causes hemolysis (hypotonic). Always add to drug or electrolyte solution before administration.

contraindications: NEVER administer undiluted as IV infusion — causes haemolysis, renal failure, and death
Not a substitute for Normal Saline or other isotonic solutions
black_box_warnings: Fatal if infused undiluted IV — causes acute intravascular haemolysis, hyperkalaemia, and death; must only be used as a vehicle for reconstitution/dilution
pediatric_specific_warnings: NEVER infuse pure WFI — fatal haemolysis risk especially in neonates
Neonates and infants: minimise free water volume to prevent hyponatraemia and fluid overload
Always verify correct diluent for each drug — some drugs require NS or D5W, not WFI
Single-use ampoules — discard unused portion
monitoring_parameters: Not applicable as standalone agent; monitor reconstituted drug parameters

administration.reconstitution: Used as the reconstitution vehicle itself — follow specific drug instructions
administration.infusion_rate_note: NEVER infuse undiluted; used only to reconstitute/dilute other medications
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with most injectable medications; verify specific drug compatibility
administration.storage: Store below 25°C; single-use — discard unused portion

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; single-use containers — discard after opening; do not use if particulate matter visible
lactation_note: Not applicable — pharmaceutical vehicle

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children and adults; widely used in pediatric ENT for otitis externa; otic formulation preferred for ear infections — avoid ophthalmic use (neomycin ototoxicity risk with perforated TM)

formulations.indian_conc_note: Neomycin C 3.5 mg/10000U/400U/10mg per mL / 5 mL
Neomycin C 3.5 mg/5000U/400U/10mg per g / 5 g

dosing_bands.notes: Thin layer TDS x 7 days max. Do not use on face. Steroid component — risk of skin atrophy with prolonged use.

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to neomycin or other aminoglycosides
Viral infections (herpes, varicella) — steroid may worsen
Fungal skin infections (unless combined with antifungal)
cross_reactions: Contact dermatitis to neomycin (common — up to 6% of treated patients)

interactions.effect: Additive ototoxicity and nephrotoxicity if significant systemic absorption from large wounds

pediatric_specific_warnings: ALWAYS confirm intact tympanic membrane before otic use — neomycin causes sensorineural hearing loss if it reaches middle ear
Limit duration to 7-10 days — prolonged steroid use causes skin atrophy
Neomycin contact allergy is common — if rash worsens, suspect allergy and discontinue
Avoid on large body surface area in infants — risk of systemic aminoglycoside absorption
Do not use ophthalmic — neomycin is toxic to corneal epithelium with prolonged use
monitoring_parameters: Signs of contact allergy (worsening rash, pruritus — neomycin allergy)
Hearing (if prolonged ear use or perforated TM suspected)

administration.storage: Store below 25°C; discard 4 weeks after opening

storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; discard ear drops 4 weeks after opening; ointment: use within 6 months of opening
lactation_note: Minimal systemic absorption from topical/otic use; safe during lactation

drug_class: Super-potent corticosteroid + aminoglycoside antibiotic + azole antifungal combination (topical)
unlicensed_note: Clobetasol is a super-potent (Group I) steroid — NOT recommended for routine pediatric use; use only under dermatology specialist guidance for short duration; widely misused in India for non-specific dermatoses

dosing_bands.notes: Super-potent steroid + antibiotic + antifungal. MAX 7 days. NOT for face/groin. Switch to milder combination after 7 days.

contraindications: Primary viral (herpes, varicella), bacterial, or fungal skin infections (monotherapy)

interactions.effect: Additive skin atrophy risk if used on overlapping areas

pediatric_specific_warnings: SUPER-POTENT (Group I) steroid — children are at higher risk of systemic absorption due to higher BSA:weight ratio
NEVER use on face, genitals, flexures, or diaper area
Maximum duration 7 days in children — risk of irreversible skin atrophy and striae
HPA axis suppression possible even with short-term use on large areas
Avoid under occlusion (diapers act as occlusive dressings)
First-line: use hydrocortisone 1% or mometasone 0.1% based combinations instead
Widely misused in India — educate parents about steroid skin damage
monitoring_parameters: Signs of skin atrophy (thinning, telangiectasia, striae)
HPA axis suppression if used on large areas or under occlusion in children (morning cortisol)
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; do not freeze; close cap tightly after use
lactation_note: Avoid application on breast area; clobetasol is super-potent — systemic absorption risk
reference_source: Indian Association of Dermatologists (IADVL) Advisory on Irrational FDC Topicals
